title: tdd run-state checkpoint
description: Eagerly-written, branch-committed checkpoint that lets a run resume after a limit-pause (Claude or Codex) without losing progress. Lives at .tdd/<slug>/run-state.json. See commands/run.md -> 'Limits, checkpointing & resume'.

run_id.description: Unique id for this run (stable across resumes).

slug.description: Feature slug; the run lives on feature/<slug>.

epic.description: The integration/epic branch this feature targets (e.g. feature/flex-cis).

base_tip.description: origin/<epic> commit sha the run started from (provenance anchor).

status.description: Run-level status. A resume that finds 'complete' no-ops and self-terminates the schedule.

paused.description: Set when status is paused-on-limit; null otherwise.

service.description: Which service's limit caused the pause.

retry_after.description: ISO timestamp the limit error said to retry after, if any; a smart scheduler prefers this over blind hourly.

slices.description: Per-slice progress. A resume skips 'integrated', runs only the owed verification for 'green-unverified', and continues 'in_progress'/'pending'.

status.description: green-unverified = arbiter passed but the cross-model verification is still owed (e.g. paused on a Codex limit); NOT integrated.

iterations.description: Iteration counter, so the circuit breaker (max 3) survives a resume.

parked_reason.description: Set when status is parked: spec-gap | breaker | divergence.

code_tip.description: SHA of the slice's code track branch tip — so a resume continues 'in_progress' from the exact committed point, not a guess.

test_tip.description: SHA of the slice's test track branch tip.

wave.description: Dependency-DAG wave this slice belongs to.

arbiter_verdict.description: The arbiter's owed GREEN for a 'green-unverified' slice — so a resume runs ONLY the pending cross-model verification, not the whole slice.

verified_diff.description: Hash/ref of the assembled diff the verdict was made against — a resume re-verifies the same tree it paused on, and can detect drift.

integrated.description: Ids of slices merged into feature/<slug>.

escalations.description: Path to .tdd/<slug>/escalations.md (genuine ambiguity only — never limit-pauses).

updated_at.description: ISO timestamp of this checkpoint write.

lock.description: Mutual-exclusion lease so two concurrent resumes (e.g. overlapping cron fires) cannot run the same slug at once. The ATOMIC primitive is a git ref refs/tdd/lock/<slug> created via compare-and-swap; this records it for visibility. A resume refuses to start while a live (unexpired) lock is held; it may steal an expired one.

acquired_at.description: ISO timestamp the lease was taken.

expires_at.description: ISO timestamp; after this a resume may steal the lease (the holder is presumed dead).
